title: The Move to AI Agents
verdict: All 8 checks pass. Reading is clean, well-structured, and beginner-appropriate. No issues found.

format_discipline.notes: First non-empty line is '# The Move to AI Agents' — no frontmatter. Sidecar reading.meta.json exists and parses. Exactly 6 sections in correct order.

length_budget.notes: 1372 words (range 1200–1600). 7 minutes at 230 wpm (range 6–8). Both within bounds.

citation_discipline.notes: All three resources [1][2][3] are cited inline and appear in the References section bibliography. No orphans in either direction.

no_forward_references.notes: No references to future topic IDs (3.5, 4.3), multi-agent architectures, RAG, or named frameworks (LangChain/AutoGen/CrewAI). The diagram embed (./diagram.mmd) is an artifact cross-reference, not a topic forward reference. The corpus mentions LangChain/AutoGen/CrewAI with a future-topic deferral, but this language was correctly excluded from the reading.

sampled_assertions.assertion: An AI agent breaks that limit by giving the LLM a goal to pursue across many steps, tools to act on the world, and memory to keep track of progress.
Think of a plain LLM as a brilliant expert locked in a room with no phone, no notebook...
Five components: Goal, Planning, Tool use, Memory, Feedback loop
API definition: a standardised way for one software system to request something from another
sampled_assertions.corpus_support: Corpus §5.3 mentions API calls; reading adds plain-language definition consistent with corpus intent — no contradiction

sampled_assertions.assertion: Short-term memory held in the LLM's context window, long-term memory persists across sessions
Hallucination propagates / Goal ambiguity / Error compounding / Cost as limitations
sampled_assertions.corpus_support: Corpus §5.4: all four named and described — confirmed

sampled_assertions.assertion: Worked example: stock price + recent news summary scenario
Customer support, Research and reporting, Software development as real-world patterns
sampled_assertions.corpus_support: Corpus §7: all three patterns present — confirmed

sampled_assertions.assertion: System prompt: instructions LLM receives before the user's goal
Keep humans in the loop for irreversible actions

faithfulness.notes: All 10 sampled assertions trace directly to the corpus. No hallucinated or unsupported claims detected.

beginner_readability.notes: LLM defined at first use (line 5). API expanded inline at tool-use section. All non-everyday terms defined before or at first use. Analogy (expert locked in a room) precedes the technical definition — concrete-first pattern maintained. No academic hedging detected. Average sentence length is well under 25 words throughout.

formatting.notes: Paragraphs are 2–4 sentences throughout. Agent loop steps are in a numbered list (lines 61–66). Worked Example is step-by-step numbered. Mumbai-to-London example in Key Concepts is inline and concrete. Bold terms at definition points: Goal, Planning, Tool use, Memory, feedback loop all bolded at introduction. Comparison table enhances scannability.

assessment_reference.notes: No mention of A1, due W3, 10%, or any assessment metadata in the reading.

scope.notes: Reading stays within single-agent loop concepts throughout. Multi-agent architectures (deferred to 3.5) not mentioned. RAG (deferred to 4.3) not mentioned. Named frameworks (LangChain, AutoGen, CrewAI) correctly excluded from the reading — present only in corpus §6 which was not surfaced in the artifact. In Practice section correctly covers single-agent deployment patterns only.

hard_fail_escalation.reason: No scope_creep found; checks 5–6 produced 0 distinct soft findings — escalation threshold not reached.

gold_example_comparison.notes: No gold example found at .claude/references/gold-examples/reading/ — comparison skipped.
